message.content:
Read these files in full from /Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile and return their COMPLETE contents (don't truncate):

1. services/handset/internal/jobs/expired_contracts.go
2. services/handset/internal/jobs/jobs.go
3. services/handset/internal/jobs/expired_contracts_test.go
4. services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go — only the `FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts` and `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved` functions and the struct definition with nowFunc
5. services/handset/internal/storage/storage_test.go — only the new test cases added (look for FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts and ExpiresAt related tests)
6. services/handset/cmd/handset/jobs.go
7. services/handset/internal/models/models.go
8. services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer_test.go — only the new ErrConflict test case

Return the full code for each. Also run `git diff main...HEAD -- services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go` to see exactly what changed in storage.go.

attachment.content:
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	package jobs
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/google/uuid"
9		. "github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2"
10		. "github.com/onsi/gomega"
11		"go.uber.org/mock/gomock"
12		"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb"
13	
14		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/generated/protos/mobile/v1/events/serviceevents"
15		mockJobs "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/jobs/mocks"
16		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/models"
17	)
18	
19	var _ = Describe("expireContractsRunner", func() {
20		var (
21			ctrl         *gomock.Controller
22			mockStore    *mockJobs.MockStorage
23			mockProducer *mockJobs.MockProducer
24			ctx          context.Context
25			runnerFunc   func(ctx context.Context)
26		)
27	
28		BeforeEach(func() {
29			ctrl = gomock.NewController(GinkgoT())
30			mockStore = mockJobs.NewMockStorage(ctrl)
31			mockProducer = mockJobs.NewMockProducer(ctrl)
32			ctx = context.Background()
33			runnerFunc = expireContractsRunner(mockStore, mockProducer)
34		})
35	
36		AfterEach(func() {
37			ctrl.Finish()
38		})
39	
40		When("no contracts are expired", func() {
41			It("does not produce any events", func() {
42				mockStore.EXPECT().FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts(gomock.Any()).
43					Return([]models.Contract{}, nil)
44				// No producer call expected
45				runnerFunc(ctx)
46			})
47		})
48	
49		When("an error occurs before any contracts have been marked as expired", func() {
50			It("does not produce any events", func() {
51				mockStore.EXPECT().FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts(gomock.Any()).
52					Return([]models.Contract{}, errors.New("badness"))
53				// No producer call expected
54				runnerFunc(ctx)
55			})
56		})
57	
58		// Helper function to create a bunch of contracts with expected producer calls
59		setupContracts := func(count int, store mockJobs.MockStorage, producer mockJobs.MockProducer) []models.Contract {
60			now := time.Now()
61			contracts := make([]models.Contract, count)
62			for i := range count {
63				id, err := uuid.NewRandom()
64				Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
65	
66				contracts[i] = models.Contract{
67					ID:         id,
68					ServiceID:  "service-" + id.String(),
69					CustomerID: "customer-" + id.String(),
70					HandsetID:  "handset-" + id.String(),
71					ExpiresAt:  &now,
72					Status:     models.ContractStatusExpired,
73				}
74	
75				mockProducer.EXPECT().ProduceMobileHandsetContractExpiredSync(gomock.Any(), &serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractExpired{
76					ServiceId:  "service-" + id.String(),
77					CustomerId: "customer-" + id.String(),
78					ContractId: id.String(),
79					HandsetId:  "handset-" + id.String(),
80					ExpiresAt:  timestamppb.New(now),
81				})
82			}
83	
84			return contracts
85		}
86	
87		When("contracts have been expired with no errors", func() {
88			It("produces one event per contract", func() {
89				contracts := setupContracts(10, *mockStore, *mockProducer)
90				mockStore.EXPECT().FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts(gomock.Any()).
91					Return(contracts, nil)
92	
93				runnerFunc(ctx)
94			})
95		})
96	
97		When("contracts have been expired with an error", func() {
98			It("still does produces one event per contract", func() {
99				contracts := setupContracts(100, *mockStore, *mockProducer)
100				mockStore.EXPECT().FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts(gomock.Any()).
101					Return(contracts, errors.New("badness"))
102	
103				runnerFunc(ctx)
104			})
105		})
106	})
107

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"os"
6		"time"
7	
8		cli "github.com/jawher/mow.cli"
9		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/protoc-gen-protostrate/protostrate"
10		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/protoc-gen-protostrate/protostrate/interceptor/producer"
11		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/protoc-gen-protostrate/protostrate_tools"
12		protostrate_instrumented "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-monorepo/pkg/prometheus/protostrate"
13		"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup"
14	
15		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/generated/protos/mobile/v1/events/serviceevents"
16		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tbootstrap"
17		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tlog"
18		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tpubsub"
19		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tstorage/tmongo"
20		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tvars"
21		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/events"
22		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/jobs"
23		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage"
24	)
25	
26	func startJobsMode(ctx context.Context, eg *errgroup.Group, app *cli.Cli, cfg *sharedConfig) {
27		kafkaTopic := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
28			Name:   "kafka-topic-out",
29			Desc:   "Kafka topic to publish lifecycle events to",
30			EnvVar: "KAFKA_TOPIC_OUT",
31		})
32	
33		mongoDB, err := tmongo.InitMongoDB(ctx, tmongo.MongoOpts{
34			Url:          *cfg.mongoURL,
35			DatabaseName: *cfg.mongoDBName,
36		})
37		if err != nil {
38			tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "failed to initialise mongodb")
39		}
40	
41		db, err := storage.NewStorage(ctx, *mongoDB, time.Now)
42		if err != nil {
43			tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "failed to create mongo store")
44		}
45	
46		// Kafka producer for lifecycle events
47		kafkaSink := tpubsub.InitAsyncSink(ctx, tpubsub.PubSubSinkOpts{
48			PubSubOpts: tpubsub.PubSubOpts{
49				AppName: appName,
50				Brokers: *cfg.kafkaBrokers,
51				Topic:   *kafkaTopic,
52				Version: *cfg.kafkaVersion,
53			},
54		})
55		defer kafkaSink.Sink.Close()
56	
57		protostrateProd := protostrate.NewProducer(
58			protostrate_tools.NewAsyncSubstrateProducerDestination(kafkaSink.Sink),
59			protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewSenderDecoratorInterceptor(appName, tvars.Namespace, "")),
60			protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(protostrate_instrumented.NewProducerMetrics()),
61			protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewTokenCorrelationInterceptor()),
62			protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewAuditInterceptor()),
63			tpubsub.WithProducerEnvelopeTranslator(),
64		)
65		defer protostrateProd.Close()
66		go func() {
67			if err := protostrateProd.Produce(ctx); err != nil {
68				tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "stopped producing messages")
69			}
70		}()
71	
72		eventProducer := events.NewProducer(serviceevents.NewServiceeventsProducer(protostrateProd))
73	
74		// Setup scheduler for handset updates
75		tlog.Info(ctx, "Setup scheduler for handset updates")
76		jobs := jobs.New(ctx, db, eventProducer)
77		jobs.Start()
78		defer jobs.Stop()
79	
80		opsServer := tbootstrap.InitOpsServer(ctx, tbootstrap.OpsServerOpts{
81			AppName:        appName,
82			AppDescription: appDesc,
83			Revision:       gitHash,
84			Addr:           *cfg.opsAddress,
85		})
86	
87		err = tbootstrap.StartBootstrapGroup(eg,
88			tbootstrap.WithMongoClient(ctx, *mongoDB),
89			tbootstrap.WithProtostrateProducer(ctx, protostrateProd, *kafkaTopic),
90			tbootstrap.WithOpsServer(ctx, opsServer),
91		)
92		if err != nil {
93			tlog.WithError(err).Error(ctx, "error returned from error group")
94			os.Exit(1)
95		}
96	}
97

1	package models
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		"github.com/google/uuid"
7	)
8	
9	type ContractStatus string
10	
11	const (
12		ContractStatusPendingEligibility  ContractStatus = "PENDING_ELIGIBILITY"
13		ContractStatusEligibilityApproved ContractStatus = "ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED"
14		ContractStatusApproved            ContractStatus = "APPROVED"
15		ContractStatusDeclined            ContractStatus = "DECLINED"
16		ContractStatusHandsetOrdered      ContractStatus = "HANDSET_ORDERED"
17		ContractStatusExpired             ContractStatus = "EXPIRED"
18	)
19	
20	type Handset struct {
21		ID                uuid.UUID           `json:"id" bson:"id"`
22		BillID            string              `json:"billID" bson:"billID"`
23		Active            bool                `json:"active" bson:"active"`
24		QuantityInStock   int                 `json:"quantityInStock" bson:"quantityInStock"`
25		CurrentProperties HandsetProperties   `json:"currentProperties" bson:"currentProperties"`
26		Versions          []HandsetProperties `json:"versions" bson:"versions"`
27	}
28	
29	type HandsetProperties struct {
30		Version            string    `bson:"version" json:"version"`
31		Name               string    `bson:"name" json:"name"`
32		RentalPricePence   int       `bson:"rentalPricePence" json:"rentalPricePence"`
33		RRPPence           int       `bson:"rrpPence" json:"rrpPence"`
34		ContractTermMonths int       `bson:"contractTermMonths" json:"contractTermMonths"`
35		Manufacturer       string    `bson:"manufacturer" json:"manufacturer"`
36		Model              string    `bson:"model" json:"model"`
37		Colours            string    `bson:"colours" json:"colours"`
38		Capacity           string    `bson:"capacity" json:"capacity"`
39		ScreenSize         string    `bson:"screenSize" json:"screenSize"`
40		Battery            string    `bson:"battery" json:"battery"`
41		SDCapacity         string    `bson:"sdCapacity" json:"sdCapacity"`
42		WiFiStandard       string    `bson:"wiFiStandard" json:"wiFiStandard"`
43		ESim               bool      `bson:"eSim" json:"eSim"`
44		DualSim            bool      `bson:"dualSim" json:"dualSim"`
45		SaleStatus         string    `bson:"saleStatus" json:"saleStatus"`
46		IsNew              bool      `bson:"isNew" json:"isNew"`
47		UWLaunchDate       time.Time `bson:"uwLaunchDate" json:"uwLaunchDate"`
48		PriceChange        bool      `bson:"priceChange" json:"priceChange"`
49		EndOfLife          bool      `bson:"endOfLife" json:"endOfLife"`
50		SpecificationsLink string    `bson:"specificationsLink" json:"specificationsLink"`
51		BillID             string    `bson:"billID" json:"billID"`
52	}
53	
54	type Contract struct {
55		ID                 uuid.UUID  `bson:"id"`
56		ServiceID          string     `bson:"serviceId"`
57		CustomerID         string     `bson:"customerId"`
58		HandsetID          string     `bson:"handsetId"`
59		HandsetName        string     `bson:"handsetName"`
60		HandsetVersion     string     `bson:"handsetVersion"`
61		ContractPrice      string     `bson:"contractPrice"`
62		RetailPrice        string     `bson:"retailPrice"`
63		ContractMonths     string     `bson:"contractMonths"`
64		RequestID          string     `bson:"requestId"`
65		LoanReference      string     `bson:"loanReference,omitempty"`
66		BankAccountNumber  string     `bson:"bankAccountNumber,omitempty"`
67		BankTitle          string     `bson:"bankTitle,omitempty"`
68		Decision           string     `bson:"decision,omitempty"`
69		AgreementID        string     `bson:"agreementId,omitempty"`
70		DocusignEnvelopeID string     `bson:"docusignEnvelopeId,omitempty"`
71		SignedAt           *time.Time `bson:"signedAt,omitempty"`
72		// This field is calculated when eligibility is approved
73		ExpiresAt      *time.Time     `bson:"expiresAt,omitempty"`
74		CompletedAt    *time.Time     `bson:"completedAt,omitempty"`
75		FailureReason  string         `bson:"failureReason,omitempty"`
76		FailureStage   string         `bson:"failureStage,omitempty"`
77		FailureMessage string         `bson:"failureMessage,omitempty"`
78		FailedAt       *time.Time     `bson:"failedAt,omitempty"`
79		Status         ContractStatus `bson:"status"`
80		CreatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"createdAt"`
81		UpdatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"updatedAt"`
82	}
83	
84	type EligibilityOutcome struct {
85		Decision           string
86		AgreementID        string
87		DocusignEnvelopeID string
88		SignedAt           *time.Time
89		CompletedAt        *time.Time
90	}
91	
92	type EligibilityFailureOutcome struct {
93		FailureReason  string
94		FailureStage   string
95		FailureMessage string
96		FailedAt       *time.Time
97	}
98

1	package storage_test
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		. "github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2"
9		. "github.com/onsi/gomega"
10		"go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/bson"
11		"go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo"
12		"go.uber.org/mock/gomock"
13	
14		"github.com/google/uuid"
15		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/terrors"
16		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/models"
17		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage"
18		mockStorage "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage/mocks"
19	)
20	
21	var testContractUUID = uuid.MustParse("22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222")
22	
23	var _ = Describe("Contract Storage", func() {
24		var (
25			ctrl          *gomock.Controller
26			mockContracts *mockStorage.MockMongoCollection
27			ctx           context.Context
28			store         *storage.StorageWrapper
29		)
30	
31		BeforeEach(func() {
32			ctrl = gomock.NewController(GinkgoT())
33			mockContracts = mockStorage.NewMockMongoCollection(ctrl)
34			store = storage.NewStorageWithCollections(mockContracts, mockContracts, time.Now)
35			ctx = context.Background()
36		})
37	
38		AfterEach(func() {
39			ctrl.Finish()
40		})
41	
42		Describe("CreateContract", func() {
43			It("inserts a contract into the collection", func() {
44				contract := models.Contract{
45					ID:         testContractUUID,
46					ServiceID:  "service-1",
47					CustomerID: "customer-1",
48					HandsetID:  "handset-1",
49					RequestID:  "request-1",
50					Status:     models.ContractStatusPendingEligibility,
51					CreatedAt:  time.Now(),
52					UpdatedAt:  time.Now(),
53				}
54	
55				mockContracts.EXPECT().
56					InsertOne(gomock.Any(), contract).
57					Return(&mongo.InsertOneResult{}, nil)
58	
59				err := store.CreateContract(ctx, contract)
60				Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
61			})
62	
63			When("the insert fails", func() {
64				It("returns an error", func() {
65					contract := models.Contract{
66						ID:        testContractUUID,
67						RequestID: "request-1",
68						Status:    models.ContractStatusPendingEligibility,
69					}
70	
71					mockContracts.EXPECT().
72						InsertOne(gomock.Any(), contract).
73						Return(nil, errors.New("duplicate key"))
74	
75					err := store.CreateContract(ctx, contract)
76					Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
77					Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring("could not create contract"))
78				})
79			})
80		})
81	
82		Describe("GetContractByRequestID", func() {
83			It("returns the contract matching the request ID", func() {
84				expectedContract := models.Contract{
85					ID:         testContractUUID,
86					ServiceID:  "service-1",
87					CustomerID: "customer-1",
88					HandsetID:  "handset-1",
89					RequestID:  "request-1",
90					Status:     models.ContractStatusPendingEligibility,
91				}
92	
93				singleResult := mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(expectedContract, nil, nil)
94	
95				mockContracts.EXPECT().
96					FindOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
97					Return(singleResult)
98	
99				contract, err := store.GetContractByRequestID(ctx, "request-1")
100				Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
101				Expect(contract).ToNot(BeNil())
102				Expect(contract.ID).To(Equal(testContractUUID))
103				Expect(contract.RequestID).To(Equal("request-1"))
104				Expect(contract.Status).To(Equal(models.ContractStatusPendingEligibility))
105			})
106	
107			When("no contract is found", func() {
108				It("returns ErrContractNotFound", func() {
109					singleResult := mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(models.Contract{}, mongo.ErrNoDocuments, nil)
110	
111					mockContracts.EXPECT().
112						FindOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
113						Return(singleResult)
114	
115					contract, err := store.GetContractByRequestID(ctx, "nonexistent-request")
116					Expect(err).To(MatchError(terrors.ErrNotFound))
117					Expect(contract).To(BeNil())
118				})
119			})
120		})
121	
122		Describe("GetActiveContractForService", func() {
123			It("returns an active contract for the service", func() {
124				expectedContract := models.Contract{
125					ID:        testContractUUID,
126					ServiceID: "service-1",
127					Status:    models.ContractStatusApproved,
128				}
129	
130				singleResult := mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(expectedContract, nil, nil)
131	
132				mockContracts.EXPECT().
133					FindOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
134					Return(singleResult)
135	
136				contract, err := store.GetActiveContractForService(ctx, "service-1")
137				Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
138				Expect(contract).ToNot(BeNil())
139				Expect(contract.ServiceID).To(Equal("service-1"))
140			})
141	
142			When("no active contract exists", func() {
143				It("returns ErrContractNotFound", func() {
144					singleResult := mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(models.Contract{}, mongo.ErrNoDocuments, nil)
145	
146					mockContracts.EXPECT().
147						FindOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
148						Return(singleResult)
149	
150					contract, err := store.GetActiveContractForService(ctx, "service-1")
151					Expect(err).To(MatchError(terrors.ErrNotFound))
152					Expect(contract).To(BeNil())
153				})
154			})
155		})
156	
157		Describe("FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts", func() {
158			var (
159				testNow time.Time
160				nowFunc func() time.Time
161			)
162	
163			BeforeEach(func() {
164				testNow = time.Date(2025, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
165				nowFunc = func() time.Time { return testNow }
166				store = storage.NewStorageWithCollections(mockContracts, mockContracts, nowFunc)
167			})
168	
169			When("no expired contracts exist", func() {
170				It("returns an empty list", func() {
171					mockContracts.EXPECT().
172						FindOneAndUpdate(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
173						Return(mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(models.Contract{}, mongo.ErrNoDocuments, nil))
174	
175					contracts, err := store.FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts(ctx)
176					Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
177					Expect(contracts).To(BeEmpty())
178				})
179			})
180	
181			When("multiple expired contracts exist", func() {
182				It("returns all expired contracts", func() {
183					contract1 := models.Contract{ID: testContractUUID, Status: models.ContractStatusExpired}
184					contract2UUID := uuid.MustParse("33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333333333")
185					contract2 := models.Contract{ID: contract2UUID, Status: models.ContractStatusExpired}
186	
187					gomock.InOrder(
188						mockContracts.EXPECT().
189							FindOneAndUpdate(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
190							Return(mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(contract1, nil, nil)),
191						mockContracts.EXPECT().
192							FindOneAndUpdate(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
193							Return(mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(contract2, nil, nil)),
194						mockContracts.EXPECT().
195							FindOneAndUpdate(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
196							Return(mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(models.Contract{}, mongo.ErrNoDocuments, nil)),
197					)
198	
199					contracts, err := store.FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts(ctx)
200					Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
201					Expect(contracts).To(HaveLen(2))
202					Expect(contracts[0].ID).To(Equal(testContractUUID))
203					Expect(contracts[1].ID).To(Equal(contract2UUID))
204				})
205			})
206	
207			When("an error occurs mid-loop", func() {
208				It("returns partial results alongside the error", func() {
209					contract1 := models.Contract{ID: testContractUUID, Status: models.ContractStatusExpired}
210	
211					gomock.InOrder(
212						mockContracts.EXPECT().
213							FindOneAndUpdate(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
214							Return(mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(contract1, nil, nil)),
215						mockContracts.EXPECT().
216							FindOneAndUpdate(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
217							Return(mongo.NewSingleResultFromDocument(models.Contract{}, errors.New("connection error"), nil)),
218					)
219	
220					contracts, err := store.FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts(ctx)
221					Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
222					Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring("could not expire contract"))
223					Expect(contracts).To(HaveLen(1))
224					Expect(contracts[0].ID).To(Equal(testContractUUID))
225				})
226			})
227		})
228	
229		Describe("UpdateContractEligibilityApproved", func() {
230			When("signedAt is set", func() {
231				It("sends a pipeline update with expiresAt computed via $dateAdd on $contractMonths", func() {
232					signedAt := time.Date(2024, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
233	
234					mockContracts.EXPECT().
235						UpdateOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
236						DoAndReturn(func(_ context.Context, _, update any, _ ...any) (*mongo.UpdateResult, error) {
237							pipeline, ok := update.(mongo.Pipeline)
238							Expect(ok).To(BeTrue(), "expected a pipeline update when signedAt is set")
239							setFields := pipeline[0][0].Value.(bson.M)
240							Expect(setFields["signedAt"]).To(Equal(&signedAt))
241							Expect(setFields["expiresAt"]).To(Equal(bson.M{
242								"$dateAdd": bson.M{
243									"startDate": &signedAt,
244									"unit":      "month",
245									"amount":    bson.M{"$toInt": "$contractMonths"},
246								},
247							}))
248							return &mongo.UpdateResult{MatchedCount: 1}, nil
249						})
250	
251					err := store.UpdateContractEligibilityApproved(ctx, "request-1", models.EligibilityOutcome{
252						SignedAt: &signedAt,
253					})
254					Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
255				})
256			})
257	
258			When("signedAt is nil", func() {
259				It("sends a regular update without expiresAt", func() {
260					mockContracts.EXPECT().
261						UpdateOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
262						DoAndReturn(func(_ context.Context, _, update any, _ ...any) (*mongo.UpdateResult, error) {
263							setFields := update.(bson.M)["$set"].(bson.M)
264							Expect(setFields).NotTo(HaveKey("expiresAt"))
265							return &mongo.UpdateResult{MatchedCount: 1}, nil
266						})
267	
268					err := store.UpdateContractEligibilityApproved(ctx, "request-1", models.EligibilityOutcome{
269						SignedAt: nil,
270					})
271					Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
272				})
273			})
274		})
275	
276		Describe("UpdateContractStatus", func() {
277			It("updates the contract status", func() {
278				mockContracts.EXPECT().
279					UpdateOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
280					Return(&mongo.UpdateResult{MatchedCount: 1, ModifiedCount: 1}, nil)
281	
282				err := store.UpdateContractStatus(ctx, "request-1", models.ContractStatusApproved)
283				Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
284			})
285	
286			When("no contract matches the guarded transition", func() {
287				It("returns ErrContractStatusConflict", func() {
288					mockContracts.EXPECT().
289						UpdateOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
290						Return(&mongo.UpdateResult{MatchedCount: 0}, nil)
291	
292					err := store.UpdateContractStatus(ctx, "nonexistent-request", models.ContractStatusDeclined)
293					Expect(err).To(MatchError(terrors.ErrConflict))
294				})
295			})
296	
297			When("the update fails", func() {
298				It("returns an error", func() {
299					mockContracts.EXPECT().
300						UpdateOne(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
301						Return(nil, errors.New("connection error"))
302	
303					err := store.UpdateContractStatus(ctx, "request-1", models.ContractStatusApproved)
304					Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
305					Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring("could not update contract status"))
306				})
307			})
308		})
309	})

1	package consumer_test
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5	
6		. "github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2"
7		. "github.com/onsi/gomega"
8		"go.uber.org/mock/gomock"
9	
10		"github.com/google/uuid"
11		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/protoc-gen-protostrate/protostrate/message"
12		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/generated/protos/mobile/v1/events/serviceevents"
13		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/terrors"
14		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/account-gateway/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/accountgateway"
15		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer"
16		mockConsumer "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/mocks"
17		loanboxclient "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/loanbox"
18		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/models"
19	)
20	
21	var testContractUUID = uuid.MustParse("22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222")
22	
23	var _ = Describe("ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered", func() {
24		var (
25			ctrl        *gomock.Controller
26			store       *mockConsumer.MockStorage
27			consumerSvc *consumer.Consumer
28			ctx         context.Context
29			acked       bool
30			ack         func() error
31		)
32	
33		BeforeEach(func() {
34			ctrl = gomock.NewController(GinkgoT())
35			store = mockConsumer.NewMockStorage(ctrl)
36			consumerSvc = consumer.New(store, nil, nil)
37			ctx = context.Background()
38			acked = false
39			ack = func() error {
40				acked = true
41				return nil
42			}
43		})
44	
45		consume := func(contractID string) error {
46			return consumerSvc.ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered(
47				ctx,
48				&message.Envelope{Id: "event-1"},
49				&serviceevents.MobileHandsetOrdered{ContractId: contractID},
50				ack,
51			)
52		}
53	
54		When("contract is already HANDSET_ORDERED", func() {
55			It("skips and acks", func() {
56				store.EXPECT().
57					GetContractByID(gomock.Any(), testContractUUID).
58					Return(&models.Contract{ID: testContractUUID, Status: models.ContractStatusHandsetOrdered}, nil)
59	
60				Expect(consume(testContractUUID.String())).To(Succeed())
61				Expect(acked).To(BeTrue())
62			})
63		})
64	
65		When("contract is not APPROVED", func() {
66			It("returns an error without acking", func() {
67				store.EXPECT().
68					GetContractByID(gomock.Any(), testContractUUID).
69					Return(&models.Contract{ID: testContractUUID, Status: models.ContractStatusEligibilityApproved}, nil)
70	
71				Expect(consume(testContractUUID.String())).To(HaveOccurred())
72				Expect(acked).To(BeFalse())
73			})
74		})
75	
76		When("contract is APPROVED", func() {
77			It("updates status and acks", func() {
78				store.EXPECT().
79					GetContractByID(gomock.Any(), testContractUUID).
80					Return(&models.Contract{
81						ID:     testContractUUID,
82						Status: models.ContractStatusApproved,
83					}, nil)
84	
85				store.EXPECT().
86					UpdateContractStatusByID(gomock.Any(), testContractUUID, models.ContractStatusHandsetOrdered).
87					Return(nil)
88	
89				Expect(consume(testContractUUID.String())).To(Succeed())
90				Expect(acked).To(BeTrue())
91			})
92		})
93	})
94	
95	var _ = Describe("ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved", func() {
96		var (
97			ctrl          *gomock.Controller
98			store         *mockConsumer.MockStorage
99			accountClient *mockConsumer.MockAccountClient
100			loanboxClient *mockConsumer.MockLoanboxClient
101			consumerSvc   *consumer.Consumer
102			ctx           context.Context
103			acked         bool
104			ack           func() error
105		)
106	
107		BeforeEach(func() {
108			ctrl = gomock.NewController(GinkgoT())
109			store = mockConsumer.NewMockStorage(ctrl)
110			accountClient = mockConsumer.NewMockAccountClient(ctrl)
111			loanboxClient = mockConsumer.NewMockLoanboxClient(ctrl)
112			consumerSvc = consumer.New(store, accountClient, loanboxClient)
113			ctx = context.Background()
114			acked = false
115			ack = func() error {
116				acked = true
117				return nil
118			}
119		})
120	
121		consume := func(event *serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractApproved) error {
122			return consumerSvc.ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved(
123				ctx,
124				&message.Envelope{Id: "event-1"},
125				event,
126				ack,
127			)
128		}
129	
130		When("contract is already HANDSET_ORDERED", func() {
131			It("skips and acks", func() {
132				store.EXPECT().
133					GetContractByID(gomock.Any(), testContractUUID).
134					Return(&models.Contract{ID: testContractUUID, Status: models.ContractStatusHandsetOrdered}, nil)
135	
136				Expect(consume(&serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractApproved{
137					ContractId: testContractUUID.String(),
138				})).To(Succeed())
139				Expect(acked).To(BeTrue())
140			})
141		})
142	
143		When("contract is ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED with loan values", func() {
144			It("creates loan with approved event values, updates references, and acks", func() {
145				store.EXPECT().
146					GetContractByID(gomock.Any(), testContractUUID).
147					Return(&models.Contract{
148						ID:                testContractUUID,
149						RequestID:         "request-1",
150						Status:            models.ContractStatusEligibilityApproved,
151						BankAccountNumber: "12345678",
152						BankTitle:         "Barclays",
153					}, nil)
154	
155				accountClient.EXPECT().
156					GetAccount(gomock.Any(), &accountgateway.GetAccountRequest{AccountId: "customer-1"}).
157					Return(&accountgateway.GetAccountResponse{Account: &accountgateway.Account{}}, nil)
158	
159				loanboxClient.EXPECT().
160					CreateLoan(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
161					DoAndReturn(func(_ context.Context, req loanboxclient.CreateLoanRequest) (*loanboxclient.CreateLoanResponse, error) {
162						Expect(req.AmountApproved).To(Equal(float32(950)))
163						Expect(req.TermMonths).To(Equal(int32(24)))
164						Expect(req.BankAccountNumber).To(Equal("12345678"))
165						return &loanboxclient.CreateLoanResponse{LoanApplicationID: 123}, nil
166					})
167	
168				store.EXPECT().
169					UpdateContractLoanReference(gomock.Any(), "request-1", "123").
170					Return(nil)
171	
172				store.EXPECT().
173					UpdateContractStatus(gomock.Any(), "request-1", models.ContractStatusApproved).
174					Return(nil)
175	
176				Expect(consume(&serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractApproved{
177					ServiceId:      "service-1",
178					CustomerId:     "customer-1",
179					ContractId:     testContractUUID.String(),
180					HandsetId:      "h-1",
181					RetailPrice:    &serviceevents.Money{Amount: "950", Currency: "GBP"},
182					ContractMonths: "24",
183				})).To(Succeed())
184				Expect(acked).To(BeTrue())
185			})
186		})
187	
188		When("UpdateContractStatus returns ErrConflict", func() {
189			It("acks without error", func() {
190				store.EXPECT().
191					GetContractByID(gomock.Any(), testContractUUID).
192					Return(&models.Contract{
193						ID:                testContractUUID,
194						RequestID:         "request-1",
195						Status:            models.ContractStatusEligibilityApproved,
196						BankAccountNumber: "12345678",
197						BankTitle:         "Barclays",
198					}, nil)
199	
200				accountClient.EXPECT().
201					GetAccount(gomock.Any(), &accountgateway.GetAccountRequest{AccountId: "customer-1"}).
202					Return(&accountgateway.GetAccountResponse{Account: &accountgateway.Account{}}, nil)
203	
204				loanboxClient.EXPECT().
205					CreateLoan(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).
206					Return(&loanboxclient.CreateLoanResponse{LoanApplicationID: 123}, nil)
207	
208				store.EXPECT().
209					UpdateContractLoanReference(gomock.Any(), "request-1", "123").
210					Return(nil)
211	
212				store.EXPECT().
213					UpdateContractStatus(gomock.Any(), "request-1", models.ContractStatusApproved).
214					Return(terrors.ErrConflict)
215	
216				Expect(consume(&serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractApproved{
217					ServiceId:      "service-1",
218					CustomerId:     "customer-1",
219					ContractId:     testContractUUID.String(),
220					HandsetId:      "h-1",
221					RetailPrice:    &serviceevents.Money{Amount: "950", Currency: "GBP"},
222					ContractMonths: "24",
223				})).To(Succeed())
224				Expect(acked).To(BeTrue())
225			})
226		})
227	})
